journal_entry.description: Purchase of sedan for executive use, financed through dealer payment plan
journal_entry.rationale: Vehicle asset increases by $48,000, non-recoverable sales tax of $4,800 (10% of $48,000) is capitalized to the vehicle cost for a total asset of $52,800. Since the tax is non-recoverable, it forms part of the cost of the asset. The entire amount of $52,800 is financed, creating a liability (Note Payable or Accounts Payable to Ford).

output_debit_classifier.reason: Asset acquired on credit. Vehicle (asset) increases by $52,800 total (base $48,000 + non-recoverable sales tax $4,800), financed liability increases by $52,800. Under Canadian accounting, non-recoverable sales tax is capitalized as part of the asset cost.
